commands: jq upstream handoffs and PTArcade outputs
web searches for BBN/CMB/dark photon/SIDM/supercooling constraints
python derived mass and handoff generation
warnings: No non-PTA published constraint is applied as an exclusion with current observables.
PTArcade MAP sits at y_D=0 and the y_D posterior should not be overinterpreted as an interior preference.
DBF template validity is not strict for strongest/supercooled benchmark points.
The rectangular 1sigma posterior contains beta_lambda<0 corners; physical interpretation requires a beta_lambda>0 mask.
approximations: Derived mass ranges use independent rectangular 1sigma bounds rather than posterior covariance.
m_chi range is a corner estimate restricted to beta_lambda>0 corners.
Constraint classification uses published assumptions from literature; no quantitative recast was performed.

notes: 14/14 benchmark points are supporting checks only.
Exact-zero portal assumptions dominate laboratory constraint applicability.

constraints.name: BBN expansion and dark radiation from hidden sector
constraints.description: Relevant because the FOPT occurs around T~0.04-0.14 GeV and particles are MeV-GeV scale, but no thermal-history module maps the zero-portal hidden sector to BBN observables.

mass_range.preferred_region: m_X=0.340-0.878 GeV; m_psi=0.055-0.695 GeV; m_chi~0.034-0.108 GeV where beta_lambda>0

constraints.observable: Delta N_eff during BBN; hidden-sector temperature ratio; abundances and lifetimes
constraints.applicability: Relevant because the FOPT occurs around T~0.04-0.14 GeV and particles are MeV-GeV scale, but no thermal-history module maps the zero-portal hidden sector to BBN observables.

tested_subregion.description: v_D=0.652-1.395 GeV; m_X=0.340-0.878 GeV; m_psi=0.055-0.695 GeV; m_chi~0.034-0.108 GeV where beta_lambda>0

applicability_notes: No exclusion claimed unless constraint_applied is true.
Exact zero epsilon and lambda_HP are treated as validated backend assumptions.

source.title: BBN constraints on MeV-scale dark sectors. Part I. Sterile decays
source.authors: Marco Hufnagel, Kai Schmidt-Hoberg, Sebastian Wild

constraints.name: BBN electromagnetic entropy injection and photodisintegration
constraints.description: Published EM-decay bounds assume decays into photons/electrons or other SM electromagnetic final states. With exact epsilon=0 and lambda_HP=0 in the backend, no SM EM decay channel is specified.

mass_range.preferred_region: m_X=0.340-0.878 GeV; m_psi=0.055-0.695 GeV; m_chi~0.034-0.108 GeV where beta_lambda>0

constraints.observable: electromagnetic decay lifetime; EM branching ratio; injected abundance
required_observables: would need decay widths if portal opened
missing_observables: would need decay widths if portal opened
constraints.applicability: Published EM-decay bounds assume decays into photons/electrons or other SM electromagnetic final states. With exact epsilon=0 and lambda_HP=0 in the backend, no SM EM decay channel is specified.

tested_subregion.description: same preferred mass region; no SM EM coupling

applicability_notes: No exclusion claimed unless constraint_applied is true.
Exact zero epsilon and lambda_HP are treated as validated backend assumptions.

source.title: BBN constraints on MeV-scale dark sectors. Part II. Electromagnetic decays
source.authors: Marco Hufnagel, Kai Schmidt-Hoberg, Sebastian Wild

constraints.name: CMB Delta N_eff / extra relativistic energy density
constraints.description: Planck-type Neff constraints are directly relevant only after computing the hidden-sector radiation density. The current handoffs do not provide this observable.

mass_range.preferred_region: m_X=0.340-0.878 GeV; m_psi=0.055-0.695 GeV; m_chi~0.034-0.108 GeV where beta_lambda>0

constraints.observable: Delta N_eff at recombination; temperature ratio; relativistic degrees of freedom
required_observables: dark-sector temperature ratio at neutrino decoupling/recombination
missing_observables: dark-sector temperature ratio at neutrino decoupling/recombination
constraints.applicability: Planck-type Neff constraints are directly relevant only after computing the hidden-sector radiation density. The current handoffs do not provide this observable.

tested_subregion.description: PTArcade 1sigma region; no computed Delta N_eff

applicability_notes: No exclusion claimed unless constraint_applied is true.
Exact zero epsilon and lambda_HP are treated as validated backend assumptions.

source.title: Cosmological constraints in extended parameter space from the Planck 2018 Legacy release
source.authors: Eleonora Di Valentino, Alessandro Melchiorri, Joseph Silk

constraints.name: CMB energy injection from annihilation/decay
constraints.description: CMB energy-injection bounds on light-mediator dark matter require stable DM abundance and SM-visible final states. Exact-zero portals make direct application unavailable; dark-only annihilation would need a model-specific recast.

mass_range.preferred_region: m_X=0.340-0.878 GeV; m_psi=0.055-0.695 GeV; m_chi~0.034-0.108 GeV where beta_lambda>0

constraints.observable: p_ann or decay energy injection into SM final states
available_observables: masses and couplings internal to dark sector
constraints.applicability: CMB energy-injection bounds on light-mediator dark matter require stable DM abundance and SM-visible final states. Exact-zero portals make direct application unavailable; dark-only annihilation would need a model-specific recast.

tested_subregion.description: m_psi=0.055-0.695 GeV; mediator masses from preferred region

applicability_notes: No exclusion claimed unless constraint_applied is true.
Exact zero epsilon and lambda_HP are treated as validated backend assumptions.

source.title: Strong constraints on self-interacting dark matter with light mediators
source.authors: Torsten Bringmann, Felix Kahlhoefer, Kai Schmidt-Hoberg, Parampreet Walia

constraints.name: Thermalization and decoupling between dark and visible sectors
constraints.description: With zero portal couplings the backend does not thermalize the dark and SM sectors by construction, but the FOPT calculation implicitly uses a dark-sector temperature. Cosmological constraints need the temperature-ratio history.

mass_range.preferred_region: m_X=0.340-0.878 GeV; m_psi=0.055-0.695 GeV; m_chi~0.034-0.108 GeV where beta_lambda>0

constraints.observable: Gamma/H for portal interactions; decoupling temperature; initial temperature ratio
constraints.applicability: With zero portal couplings the backend does not thermalize the dark and SM sectors by construction, but the FOPT calculation implicitly uses a dark-sector temperature. Cosmological constraints need the temperature-ratio history.
applicability_notes: No exclusion claimed unless constraint_applied is true.
Exact zero epsilon and lambda_HP are treated as validated backend assumptions.

source.title: US Cosmic Visions: New Ideas in Dark Matter 2017: Community Report

constraints.name: Dark photon visible searches in MeV-GeV range
constraints.description: Beam-dump, fixed-target, flavor and collider dark-photon limits in this mass range assume nonzero kinetic mixing with SM electromagnetic/hypercharge current. The validated backend fixes epsilon exactly zero.

mass_range.preferred_region: m_X=0.340-0.878 GeV; m_psi=0.055-0.695 GeV; m_chi~0.034-0.108 GeV where beta_lambda>0

constraints.observable: kinetic mixing epsilon; visible decay branching ratios; production cross section
constraints.applicability: Beam-dump, fixed-target, flavor and collider dark-photon limits in this mass range assume nonzero kinetic mixing with SM electromagnetic/hypercharge current. The validated backend fixes epsilon exactly zero.

tested_subregion.description: m_X overlaps MeV-GeV dark photon searches, but epsilon=0

applicability_notes: No exclusion claimed unless constraint_applied is true.
Exact zero epsilon and lambda_HP are treated as validated backend assumptions.

source.title: The Dark Photon
source.authors: Marco Fabbrichesi, Emidio Gabrielli, Gaia Lanfranchi

constraints.name: Dark photon invisible / dark matter mediator searches
constraints.description: Invisible mediator searches still require SM production through kinetic mixing or another visible portal. Exact epsilon=0 removes the production assumption.

mass_range.preferred_region: m_X=0.340-0.878 GeV; m_psi=0.055-0.695 GeV; m_chi~0.034-0.108 GeV where beta_lambda>0

constraints.observable: kinetic mixing epsilon; invisible branching ratio; production cross section
constraints.applicability: Invisible mediator searches still require SM production through kinetic mixing or another visible portal. Exact epsilon=0 removes the production assumption.

tested_subregion.description: m_X=0.340-0.878 GeV; possible X->psi psi only in subregions if kinematically open

applicability_notes: No exclusion claimed unless constraint_applied is true.
Exact zero epsilon and lambda_HP are treated as validated backend assumptions.

source.title: The Dark Photon
source.authors: Marco Fabbrichesi, Emidio Gabrielli, Gaia Lanfranchi

constraints.name: Dark scalar Higgs-portal laboratory constraints
constraints.description: Higgs exotic decays, beam dumps and direct-detection scalar-mediated bounds require Higgs/scalar mixing or another SM portal. Backend fixes lambda_HP exactly zero.

mass_range.preferred_region: m_X=0.340-0.878 GeV; m_psi=0.055-0.695 GeV; m_chi~0.034-0.108 GeV where beta_lambda>0

constraints.observable: Higgs portal lambda_HP; scalar mixing angle; visible decay widths
required_observables: scalar decay widths if portal opened
missing_observables: scalar decay widths if portal opened
constraints.applicability: Higgs exotic decays, beam dumps and direct-detection scalar-mediated bounds require Higgs/scalar mixing or another SM portal. Backend fixes lambda_HP exactly zero.
applicability_notes: No exclusion claimed unless constraint_applied is true.
Exact zero epsilon and lambda_HP are treated as validated backend assumptions.

source.title: Minimal self-interacting dark matter models with light mediator

constraints.name: Dark fermion relic abundance
constraints.description: The handoffs do not establish whether the degenerate Majorana pair is stable or constitutes all/part of dark matter. Relic constraints cannot be applied from mass alone.

mass_range.preferred_region: m_X=0.340-0.878 GeV; m_psi=0.055-0.695 GeV; m_chi~0.034-0.108 GeV where beta_lambda>0

constraints.observable: Omega_psi h^2; annihilation cross sections; stability symmetry
constraints.applicability: The handoffs do not establish whether the degenerate Majorana pair is stable or constitutes all/part of dark matter. Relic constraints cannot be applied from mass alone.

tested_subregion.description: m_psi=0.055-0.695 GeV; y_D MAP caveat at boundary

applicability_notes: No exclusion claimed unless constraint_applied is true.
Exact zero epsilon and lambda_HP are treated as validated backend assumptions.

source.title: Minimal self-interacting dark matter models with light mediator

constraints.name: Dark fermion self-interactions and structure formation
constraints.description: Light mediator self-interactions may be relevant, but applying Bullet Cluster/halo bounds needs the DM identity, abundance fraction and velocity-dependent cross section.

mass_range.preferred_region: m_X=0.340-0.878 GeV; m_psi=0.055-0.695 GeV; m_chi~0.034-0.108 GeV where beta_lambda>0

constraints.observable: sigma_T/m versus velocity; halo abundance; mediator spectrum
available_observables: masses and dark couplings in preferred region
constraints.applicability: Light mediator self-interactions may be relevant, but applying Bullet Cluster/halo bounds needs the DM identity, abundance fraction and velocity-dependent cross section.

tested_subregion.description: m_psi~0.055-0.695 GeV; m_X~0.340-0.878 GeV; m_chi sub-0.11 GeV where physical

applicability_notes: No exclusion claimed unless constraint_applied is true.
Exact zero epsilon and lambda_HP are treated as validated backend assumptions.

source.title: Minimal self-interacting dark matter models with light mediator

constraints.name: Percolation and completion under strong supercooling
constraints.description: Directly relevant because benchmark 14/14 points include very large alpha. Existing backend has an approximate percolation treatment, so this is flagged as approximate rather than an exclusion.

mass_range.preferred_region: m_X=0.340-0.878 GeV; m_psi=0.055-0.695 GeV; m_chi~0.034-0.108 GeV where beta_lambda>0

constraints.observable: percolation completion criterion; false-vacuum fraction; reheating temperature; alpha validity
required_observables: full completion diagnostic beyond backend approximation
available_observables: FOPT handoff and PTA benchmarks include alpha beta_H Treh
missing_observables: full completion diagnostic beyond backend approximation
constraints.applicability: Directly relevant because benchmark 14/14 points include very large alpha. Existing backend has an approximate percolation treatment, so this is flagged as approximate rather than an exclusion.

tested_subregion.description: benchmark alpha up to ~1.35e12; PTArcade MAP/1sigma not separately audited for completion

applicability_notes: No exclusion claimed unless constraint_applied is true.
Exact zero epsilon and lambda_HP are treated as validated backend assumptions.

source.title: Supercool subtleties of cosmological phase transitions
source.authors: Ryusuke Jinno, Thomas Konstandin, Hyeonseok Rubira, Jorinde van de Vis

constraints.name: Perturbativity, beta_lambda positivity and Coleman-Weinberg validity
constraints.description: Couplings are perturbative in the 1sigma region, but the rectangular posterior includes beta_lambda<0 corners; physical interpretation requires enforcing beta_lambda>0 and RG stability.

mass_range.preferred_region: m_X=0.340-0.878 GeV; m_psi=0.055-0.695 GeV; m_chi~0.034-0.108 GeV where beta_lambda>0

constraints.observable: g_D, y_D, beta_lambda, loop expansion, RG/Landau-pole behavior
available_observables: g_D, y_D, beta_lambda, loop expansion, RG/Landau-pole behavior
constraints.applicability: Couplings are perturbative in the 1sigma region, but the rectangular posterior includes beta_lambda<0 corners; physical interpretation requires enforcing beta_lambda>0 and RG stability.

tested_subregion.description: g_D=0.260-0.315; y_D=0.119-0.705; beta_lambda corner range -3.29e-4 to 5.97e-3

applicability_notes: No exclusion claimed unless constraint_applied is true.
Exact zero epsilon and lambda_HP are treated as validated backend assumptions.

source.title: Classically conformal B-L extended Standard Model and Higgs vacuum stability
source.authors: Alexander Farzinnia, Hong-Jian He, Jing Ren

missing_calculations.observable: dark-sector temperature ratio and decoupling temperature
validation_checks: recover SM Neff limit for no dark sector

missing_calculations.observable: species abundances and lifetimes through BBN/CMB
validation_checks: compare against published MeV mediator benchmark curves

missing_calculations.observable: portal-dependent decay widths and visible branching ratios
validation_checks: zero widths to SM when epsilon=lambda_HP=0

missing_calculations.observable: Majorana pair stability and relic abundance

validation_checks: sigma_T/m velocity grid at dwarf/MW/cluster scales

missing_calculations.observable: RG running, Landau-pole scale and beta_lambda physical mask
robust percolation/completion and wall-velocity validity diagnostics
